quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 4, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Ionic Compound Formulas and Transition Metal Behavior
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Predicting Ionic Compound Formulas, Ion Charges, Transition Metal Flexibility, Variable Valency, Properties of Ionic Compounds

questions.question: If calcium (Ca) reacts with oxygen (O), the product is:
questions.explanation: Calcium (Group 2) forms Ca²⁺ and oxygen (Group 16) forms O²⁻. One Ca²⁺ combines with one O²⁻ to form CaO (calcium oxide).

questions.question: If sodium (Na) reacts with oxygen (O), the product is:
questions.explanation: Sodium (Group 1) forms Na⁺ and oxygen (Group 16) forms O²⁻. Two Na⁺ ions combine with one O²⁻ ion to form Na₂O (sodium oxide).

questions.question: If potassium (K) reacts with sulfur (S), the product is:
questions.explanation: Potassium (Group 1) forms K⁺ and sulfur (Group 16) forms S²⁻. Two K⁺ ions combine with one S²⁻ ion to form K₂S (potassium sulfide).

questions.question: If lithium (Li) reacts with chlorine (Cl), the product is:
questions.explanation: Lithium (Group 1) forms Li⁺ and chlorine (Group 17) forms Cl⁻. One Li⁺ combines with one Cl⁻ to form LiCl (lithium chloride).

questions.question: If magnesium (Mg) reacts with chlorine (Cl), the product is:
questions.explanation: Magnesium (Group 2) forms Mg²⁺ and chlorine (Group 17) forms Cl⁻. One Mg²⁺ combines with two Cl⁻ ions to form MgCl₂ (magnesium chloride).

questions.question: If calcium (Ca) reacts with nitrogen (N), the product is:
questions.explanation: Calcium (Group 2) forms Ca²⁺ and nitrogen (Group 15) forms N³⁻. To balance charges, three Ca²⁺ (total +6) combine with two N³⁻ (total -6) to form Ca₃N₂ (calcium nitride).

questions.question: If aluminum (Al) reacts with sulfur (S), the product is:
questions.explanation: Aluminum (Group 13) forms Al³⁺ and sulfur (Group 16) forms S²⁻. To balance charges, two Al³⁺ (total +6) combine with three S²⁻ (total -6) to form Al₂S₃ (aluminum sulfide).

questions.question: If beryllium (Be) reacts with chlorine (Cl), the product is:
questions.explanation: Beryllium (Group 2) forms Be²⁺ and chlorine (Group 17) forms Cl⁻. One Be²⁺ combines with two Cl⁻ ions to form BeCl₂ (beryllium chloride).

questions.question: If strontium (Sr) reacts with oxygen (O), the product is:
questions.explanation: Strontium (Group 2) forms Sr²⁺ and oxygen (Group 16) forms O²⁻. One Sr²⁺ combines with one O²⁻ to form SrO (strontium oxide).

questions.question: If zinc (Zn) reacts with sulfur (S), the product is:
questions.explanation: Zinc typically forms Zn²⁺ and sulfur forms S²⁻. One Zn²⁺ combines with one S²⁻ to form ZnS (zinc sulfide).

questions.question: If iron atom (Later becomes Fe³⁺) reacts with chlorine (Cl), the product is:
questions.explanation: If iron forms Fe³⁺ and chlorine forms Cl⁻, three Cl⁻ ions are needed to balance one Fe³⁺, forming FeCl₃ (iron(III) chloride).

questions.question: Transition metals are chemically flexible. This means:
options: They can react with many different elements, including metals and non-metals
They can lose different numbers of electrons depending on external factors
They can be very conductive of heat and electricity, and act as insulators too
They are malleable and ductile at low temperature, and elastic at high temperature
questions.explanation: Transition metals are chemically flexible because they can lose different numbers of electrons to form ions with different charges (e.g., Fe²⁺ and Fe³⁺). This is due to the closeness in energy of their 4s and 3d subshells.

questions.question: The technical term for the chemical flexibility of transitional elements is:
questions.explanation: Variable valency is the technical term for the ability of transition metals to form ions with different charges. For example, iron can have a valency of 2 or 3, copper can have 1 or 2.

questions.question: All the following are properties of electrovalent compounds, EXCEPT:
options: They have high melting and boiling points
They conduct electricity in their solid state
They are generally soluble in water
They are formed by the transfer of electrons from a metal to a non-metal
questions.explanation: Ionic (electrovalent) compounds do NOT conduct electricity in their solid state because the ions are fixed in place in the crystal lattice. They conduct electricity when molten or dissolved in water, when ions are free to move.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an ionic compound?
questions.explanation: CO₂ (carbon dioxide) is not an ionic compound. It is a covalent compound formed by sharing electrons between carbon and oxygen atoms. NaCl, MgO, and CaF₂ are all ionic compounds formed by electron transfer.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered ionic compound formulas and transition metal behavior! 🧪⚡
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review charge balancing for compounds with polyatomic ions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on variable valency and properties of ionic compounds.
scoreMessages.0: Please review ion charges, how to predict formulas, and properties of ionic compounds.
